headline: Slovakia first agent baseline (rotation backfill). Material downgrade from published 50.0 functional toward developing band. September 26 2025 constitutional amendment recognizing only male/female sexes (rendering legal gender recognition impossible domestically); restricting adoption to married heterosexual couples; permitting health-professional refusal of abortion care on conscience grounds; mandating parental approval for comprehensive sexuality education. March 10-11 2026 UN Human Rights Committee under ICCPR examined Slovakia's fifth periodic report — Committee experts pressed Slovakia delegation on constitutional amendments, LGBTIQ+ rights, persistent Roma exclusion, shrinking civic space, sexual/reproductive rights access. Roma situation worsened since prior periodic review with sustained school-segregation pattern. Half-step down on EQU (constitutional erasure of non-binary persons + Roma school segregation), BND (constitutional restrictions on civil-union/adoption rights), INT (NHRI commitments contradicted by constitutional rollback). Composite 50.0 → 39.1; functional → developing band crossing. Recommend material downgrade with band-crossing.
key_evidence: September 26 2025: Slovakia parliament approved constitutional amendment recognizing only male/female sexes by exactly 90 votes (minimum required) in 150-seat National Council. Renders legal gender recognition impossible under domestic law; effectively erases non-binary persons.

Constitutional amendment also: restricts adoption to married heterosexual couples; permits health-professional refusal of abortion care on conscience grounds; mandates parental approval for comprehensive sexuality education in schools.

March 10-11 2026: UN Human Rights Committee under ICCPR examined Slovakia's fifth periodic report. Committee experts pressed delegation on constitutional amendments, LGBTIQ+ rights, Roma exclusion, civic-space shrinkage, sexual/reproductive rights access.
Roma school-segregation persistence: significant Roma children placed in 'special' schools alongside children with mild mental disabilities; situation worsened since prior periodic review.
58% of LGBTI persons in Slovakia experienced verbal harassment in 2022; 80% feared for safety in public spaces (FRA EU LGBTI II survey).
Amnesty International, Council of Europe Commissioner, UN Special Rapporteurs collectively expressed serious concern about Slovakia constitutional rollback (April-September 2025).
Slovakia NHRI annual report flagged constitutional amendments as raising concerns re EU human-rights compatibility.

evidence.source: Amnesty International — Slovakia Parliament's approval of draconian constitutional amendments
evidence.finding: Constitutional amendment approval characterized as 'step towards erosion of human rights'; details on LGBTI, reproductive-rights, education impacts.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Slovak constitutional change promotes anti-LGBTQ 'national identity'
evidence.finding: September 26 2025 vote details; framing as 'national identity' constitutional shift.

evidence.source: PBS NewsHour — Slovakia amends constitution to recognize only 2 sexes
evidence.finding: Constitutional amendment human-rights-concern framing; international response context.

evidence.source: CCPR Centre — Slovakia before the Human Rights Committee
evidence.finding: March 10-11 2026 ICCPR Committee examination; experts pressed delegation on constitutional amendments, Roma exclusion, civic-space, reproductive rights.

evidence.source: 76crimes — 2025 LGBTQ rights update: Anti-trans hysteria mars Europe's LGBTQ gains
evidence.finding: Slovakia in regional regression context; Roma-exclusion + LGBTI-rights cluster.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5. A1 detection: NHRI and ICCPR Committee functional, but constitutional rollback indicates state-level harm-signal channels structurally weakened. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5. E1 affective resonance: constitutional erasure of non-binary persons is fundamental empathic-regard failure; Roma school-segregation persistence sustains EMP-erosion. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5. AC2 proportionality: constitutional rollback is structurally disproportionate state action; AC4 response: post-rollback reactive accommodation rather than proactive remediation. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0. EQ2 distributional awareness: constitutional erasure of non-binary persons; Roma school-segregation worsening; conscience-based abortion refusal concentrates harm on women in conservative regions. Floor-proximate.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5. B3 scope clarity: constitutional restrictions on civil-union/adoption rights compress BND; B5 consent: minoritized populations (LGBTI, Roma, women seeking reproductive care) excluded from constitutional-amendment process. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5. AB2 transparency: ICCPR Committee examination provides residual accountability framework; AB4 reparation: no reparative framework for constitutional rollback. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5. S1 causal reasoning at constitutional level demonstrates rollback rather than systemic-harm reduction; civic-space shrinkage adds systemic dimension. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5. I1 consistency: NHRI commitments and EU human-rights commitments contradicted by constitutional rollback; I2 non-performance: gap between EU-membership commitments and operational reality is widening. Half-step down.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 39.1. Material change -10.9 points; functional → developing band crossing. No math-hygiene flag (reconstruction is exact). First-baseline downgrade with band-crossing.

negativeSignals: September 26 2025 constitutional amendment recognizing only male/female sexes; restrictions on civil-union/adoption rights; conscience-based abortion-refusal permission; parental-approval mandate for comprehensive sexuality education; Roma school-segregation persistence and worsening; ICCPR Committee concerns documented March 2026; civic-space shrinkage; LGBTI harassment baseline (58%/80%).
positiveSignals: ICCPR Committee functional examination process (residual accountability framework); NHRI annual reporting active (NHRI flagged constitutional concerns); EU membership provides external accountability scaffolding; some judicial-review functionality preserved.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / EU-member-state human-rights-regression cluster. Cross-reference: Hungary (HOLD until May 9 swearing-in), Poland (recent regression history), Czech Republic. Slovakia distinguished by September 2025 constitutional rollback at minimum-required vote threshold + ICCPR Committee examination + Roma-exclusion compound pattern.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with developing-band placement
open_actions_carried: Watch: ICCPR Committee concluding observations and Slovakia government response
Watch: any constitutional-amendment legal challenge or judicial review
Watch: LGBTI gender-recognition cases under European Court of Human Rights jurisdiction
Note: Hungary HOLD (May 9 Magyar swearing-in) — adjacent regional cluster
